prompt: DevBoss 5-hour sprint continuation: test end-to-end-loop on locally available/provided repositories, especially tdrrecherche and worktrees; start read-only/dry-run; record evidence, blockers, learning candidates, and Copilot availability; validate changed end-to-end-loop artifacts; do not deploy or destructively modify target repos.
commands_or_evidence: Target worktree /opt/data/worktrees/tdrrecherche/feat-complete-wordpress-design-samples exists on branch feat/complete-wordpress-design-samples at bde99b7 with clean status; main worktree /opt/data/repos/tdrrecherche remains on main at f45b2c9.
No .end-to-end-loop memory files were present in the target worktree; PROJECT_STATE.md and README.md were used as repo handoff context.
Scenario chosen: read-only WordPress scaffold and prototype readiness dry-run for the existing feature worktree, without editing target repo or deploying.
Tooling discovery: node v22.22.3 is available; php is not installed, so PHP syntax lint remains blocked locally.
Counts: 58 tracked files; 6 HTML files; 10 PHP files; 1 JS file; 2 CSS files.
HTML/asset check passed for index.html plus prototypes/variant-a through variant-e: no duplicate IDs, no broken internal fragment targets, and no missing relative href/src assets.
Initial broad secret-pattern scan had 3 false-positive doc references in PROJECT_STATE.md around the previously observed GitHub token/403 wording; refined credential-like scan returned 0 hits.
Local smoke server returned HTTP 200 for /, /prototypes/variant-a/, /prototypes/variant-e/, and /assets/generated/inline-image-1-8435fb501ca6.png.
git diff --check passed in the target worktree; target git status remained clean after checks.
GitHub CLI is authenticated, but Copilot CLI review is unavailable: `gh copilot -p ...` returned `! Copilot CLI not installed`.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Discover target repo state and any .end-to-end-loop memory
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Feature worktree exists at bde99b7 on feat/complete-wordpress-design-samples with clean status; no target .end-to-end-loop memory files found.
Selected WordPress scaffold/prototype readiness dry-run based on PROJECT_STATE.md and README.md handoff instructions.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Exercise the loop as far as safe with real commands/read-only checks
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Ran git discovery, JS syntax check, HTML/asset integrity check, refined secret scan, diff whitespace check, and local HTTP smoke without target writes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Record evidence, blockers, learning candidates, and Copilot availability
acceptance_criteria.evidence: This result log records command evidence, php lint blocker, refined secret-scan learning, and GitHub/Copilot availability.
Result log was added as commit-safe JSON; follow-up validation commands are recorded in the final report.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Do not live deploy or destructively modify target repos
acceptance_criteria.evidence: No deploy, commits, destructive commands, or target repo writes were performed; target status remained clean.

learning_candidates.text: For WordPress scaffold dry-runs without PHP/WordPress access, combine JS syntax checks, HTML/asset integrity checks, local HTTP smoke, refined credential scans, and explicit PHP-lint blocker reporting.
learning_candidates.reason: Portable eval pattern for static-to-WordPress handoff repos; privacy-safe and verified in this run.

learning_candidates.text: PHP lint remains locally blocked when php is not installed; do not claim WordPress theme syntax green without php -l or a WordPress/PHP CI runner.
learning_candidates.reason: Likely recurring blocker for PHP scaffold review in this environment; prevents false green reports.

learning_candidates.text: Broad secret scans can flag safe documentation references to tokens; use a refined credential-like pattern before classifying a secret finding as real.
learning_candidates.reason: Generalizable security-review learning from observed false positives; avoids noisy findings while preserving real secret checks.

learning_candidates.text: For tdrrecherche production work, get client design choice and WordPress/contact-form environment decisions before implementation or deploy.
learning_candidates.reason: Target-repo-specific next step already documented in PROJECT_STATE.md; keep in result log, not global skill memory.

copilot_findings.summary: GitHub CLI is authenticated, but the Copilot CLI review path is unavailable in this cron environment: gh copilot -p returned `! Copilot CLI not installed`.

notes: Read-only/dry-run eval of the tdrrecherche WordPress feature worktree completed. The only repo write was this commit-safe end-to-end-loop eval result log. Live deploy was not in scope.
